competitive_position.summary: Rompetrol is running a live three-way comparison: SAP Ariba (incumbent, semi-manual, high-cost renewal path), Zycus (AI-native, faster implementation, lower TCO), and GEP (peer finalist, feature-comparable). Coupa and Oracle were in the original tender but faded post-March. SAP Ariba is the structural threat (renewal inertia, colleagues favor keeping it per 28 May call) but is exposed on automation gaps, cost, and implementation speed. GEP is the active peer Zycus must outscore on AI orchestration and time-to-value. Meiramgul is the internal advocate pushing the switch; senior management opinion is unclear and the unsigned Ariba extension sits on hold. Zycus wins on intrinsic AI, modular phasing, and proven SAP-to-Zycus migrations (reference clients requested 24 Mar). The single strongest current threat is SAP Ariba incumbency bias, not GEP feature parity—overcoming internal inertia and securing executive sponsorship is the critical path.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: SAP Ariba charges a lot of costs, and the result is not what we are expecting... if people implementing 8 years, oh my god. My colleagues are believing them and want to keep Ariba, and I want to make the to-be process map that shows what Zycus can really do. Everything is manual, not fully automized.
competitors.how_we_win: Intrinsic AI orchestration (not bolted-on Anthropic), touchless automation vs Ariba's semi-manual processes, faster implementation (6-9 months vs Ariba's multi-year cycles), lower TCO (€6M year-1 savings vs Ariba renewal cost per 27 Mar ROI model). Activate by positioning Zycus as the modern S2P replacement with proven SAP-to-Zycus client migrations (Heineken, Nippon Gases case studies) and scheduling exec connect (the deal owner's manager) with CPO to validate internal Ariba fatigue and budget appetite.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: I was requested to make a one-sheet presentation for CEO showing annual subscription, implementation, and per-model cost for Zycus and for GEP.
competitors.how_we_win: Stronger AI orchestration (Merlin intake, autonomous negotiation, agentic workflows) vs GEP's traditional RFP-driven model, faster time-to-value (modular phasing, Romania pilot), and Zycus's 10-year AI R&D lead (32 patents, 50% of staff in R&D per intro call). Supply Meiramgul with GEP-vs-Zycus comparison slides (AI capabilities, implementation timeline, reference clients) before CEO presentation to differentiate.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Only Coupa and Oracle were invited in the original tender, excluding other qualified solutions like iValua and GEP, which may lead to higher prices due to lack of competitive options.
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa was in the original RFP but has not been mentioned in pricing, ROI, or exec-presentation discussions since March. Assume faded or deprioritized; Zycus and GEP are the live finalists.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.quote: Only Coupa and Oracle were invited in the original tender.
competitors.how_we_win: Oracle was in the original RFP but has not been mentioned since March. Assume faded; Zycus and GEP are the live alternatives to Ariba.

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 27 Oct 2025 - 28 May 2026 (8 transcribed buyer calls, Meiramgul attended all); explicit advocacy: 28 May call ('for me, Ariba is not the tool for the future'), 24 Mar call ('I want to show them advantages, not disadvantages'), 14 Apr call (pushed to add Zycus + GEP to tender when original invited only Coupa/Oracle); preparing CEO presentation alone (14 Apr call); no CPO/CEO intro facilitated (0 exec attendance across 9 touchpoints); senior management opinion unclear, Ariba extension on hold (14 Apr call); SF 0 buyer-side contact roles logged
champion_strength.summary: Meiramgul Kusherbayeva is a developing champion with strong technical advocacy but incomplete executive access and no proof of internal political capital. She has driven every milestone since October 2025, explicitly advocates for Zycus over Ariba, and is personally preparing the CEO presentation. However, she is a Manager of Procurement (not director or VP level) in a 300k deal requiring C-level approval, has not introduced Zycus to the CPO or CEO despite 8 months of validation, and has not shared internal docs proving budget lock or timeline commitment. Her sentiment is strongly positive, her effort is consistent, but her influence at the executive layer is unproven. Strength = developing. At risk: yes—she is staffing a complex enterprise decision alone without visible executive air cover, and senior management's opinion is 'unclear' with the Ariba extension 'on hold' per her own words (14 Apr call). The next move is to equip her for the CEO presentation (co-author the one-pager, supply reference-client intros, offer to co-present) and simultaneously run an executive connect (the deal owner's manager) with the CPO to validate her positioning and ensure she has the sponsorship to close.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the CPO (Chief Procurement Officer) and senior management (CEO level), but neither is formally identified in Salesforce contact roles (0 buyer-side contact roles on record) and neither has appeared directly on a Zycus call. Meiramgul Kusherbayeva (Manager of Procurement) owns the evaluation workstream and has driven all buyer-facing calls since October 2025, with Dorina Aruxandei (Strategic Procurement Manager) and Daniela Popa (Market Analysis & Intelligence Manager) participating heavily in demos and workshops. On the 24 Nov 2025 demo, the team stated 'head and director of procurement will review solutions before escalation to C-level approval' and 'top management focuses on quantifiable benefits and ROI' (demo notes, 24 Nov 2025). On 14 Apr 2026, Meiramgul explicitly confirmed she is preparing a one-page presentation for the CEO showing 'annual subscription, implementation cost, and per-model fees for Zycus and GEP' and noted 'senior management's willingness to sign the contract remains unclear, situation currently on hold' (14 Apr call). On 27 Mar 2026, she described the approval chain: her director reviews, then escalates to CPO, then to senior management for final commercial sign-off (27 Mar 2026 call). No direct engagement with the CPO or CEO has occurred; the evaluation is being staffed through procurement management, and budget authority rests at the C-level. The absence of executive engagement this late in a 300k deal is an anomaly and a control risk. The recommended move is an executive connect (the deal owner's manager) with the CPO to validate budget, timeline, and decision process before the internal presentation, or to attend the senior-management presentation alongside Meiramgul to field executive questions directly.

recommended_moves: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager schedules a call with the CPO within 7 days to validate budget, decision timeline, and internal positioning before the CEO presentation.

Equip Meiramgul for CEO presentation: deliver crisp 3-slide exec summary (AI orchestration advantage, proven SAP Ariba-to-Zycus client migrations with references, TCO + speed-to-value edge) and offer to co-author her one-pager or co-present at the senior-management meeting.
Finalize and deliver revised pricing proposal (annual license + implementation cost) within 3 business days to unblock senior-management presentation and RFI finalization.
